protein_name: Plasma membrane-associated small GTPase which cycles between an active GTP-bound and inactive GDP-bound state. In active state binds to a variety of effector proteins to regulate cellular responses, such as cell spreading and the formation of actin-based protusions including lamellipodia and membrane ruffles. Promotes cell adhesion and spreading on fibrinogen in a CIB1 and alpha-IIb/beta3 integrin-mediated manner. Belongs to the small GTPase superfamily. Rho family. Highest levels in brain, also detected in heart, placenta and pancreas. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.
